scenario: estrangement from a sibling after a family disagreement

turns.user_message: Hey. So I've been struggling with something family-related. My sibling and I aren't talking and I don't know if we ever will again.
I know, right? It sounds simple when I say it out loud but it doesn't feel simple.
I tried to talk about this with my therapist friend but they just said I should just get over it and move on.
Yeah. And what makes it harder is that nobody around me seems to understand.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
My my therapist friend noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with a family disagreement and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with a family disagreement and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by a family disagreement more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with a family disagreement and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with a family disagreement and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I just realized something. the a family disagreement is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.

I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
Thank you for letting me just... be messy about this. I needed that.
